 {"id":"bd-kbgh","title":"Implement ntm_kill_session() and ntm_interrupt_session()","description":"# Session Cleanup Functions

## Parent Epic: bd-9o2h (NTM Driver Integration Layer)

## Purpose
Cleanup sessions after processing or on error/interrupt.

## Implementation

```bash
ntm_kill_session() {
    local session="$1"
    ntm kill "$session" -f 2>/dev/null || true
}

ntm_interrupt_session() {
    local session="$1"
    ntm --robot-interrupt="$session" 2>/dev/null || true
}
```

## Kill Session
- Force kills the tmux session
- -f flag prevents confirmation prompt
- Idempotent (safe to call on non-existent session)
- Used after workflow completes or on fatal error

## Interrupt Session
- Sends Ctrl+C to agent panes
- Used to stop long-running agent work
- Can be followed by prompt retry

## Cleanup Pattern
```bash
cleanup_agent_sweep_sessions() {
    [[ "${AGENT_SWEEP_KEEP_SESSIONS:-false}" == "true" ]] && return 0
    
    local sessions
    sessions=$(ntm --robot-status 2>/dev/null | \
        grep -o '"name":"ru_sweep_[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
    for session in $sessions; do
        # Only kill sessions from this PID
        if [[ "$session" == *"_$$"* ]] || [[ "$session" == *"_$$_"* ]]; then
            ntm_kill_session "$session"
        fi
    done
}
```

## Session Preservation
Respects these flags:
- --keep-sessions: Never kill sessions
- --keep-sessions-on-fail: Keep failed repo sessions (default true)

## Trap Handler
```bash
trap 'cleanup_agent_sweep_sessions; save_agent_sweep_state "interrupted"' INT TERM
```

## Considerations
- Always capture pane output BEFORE killing session
- Capture artifacts for debugging failed repos
- Return true even on failure (cleanup should not fail sweep)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:50:13.532543977-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T19:05:33.945444761-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T19:05:33.945444761-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented ntm_kill_session(), ntm_interrupt_session(), and cleanup_agent_sweep_sessions() at lines 6246-6282."}
 {"id":"bd-kczb","title":"Implement cmd_agent_sweep() main function","description":"# Agent Sweep Main Command Function

## Parent Epic: bd-mkoc (Agent Sweep Command Implementation)

## Purpose
Main entry point for `ru agent-sweep` command.

## Implementation Structure

```bash
cmd_agent_sweep() {
    local with_release=false
    local parallel=1
    local repos_filter=""
    local dry_run=false
    local resume=false
    local restart=false

    # Parse arguments (while loop with case)
    # ... --with-release, -j N, --repos=, --dry-run, etc.

    # ADDED: Concurrent instance lock (prevent multiple agent-sweep runs)
    local lock_dir="${AGENT_SWEEP_STATE_DIR}/instance.lock"
    if ! mkdir "$lock_dir" 2>/dev/null; then
        local existing_pid
        existing_pid=$(cat "$lock_dir/pid" 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
        log_error "Another agent-sweep is already running (PID: $existing_pid)"
        log_error "If stale, remove: $lock_dir"
        return 1
    fi
    echo $$ > "$lock_dir/pid"
    trap 'rmdir "$lock_dir" 2>/dev/null || true' EXIT

    # Check ntm availability (fail fast)
    ntm_check_available || { log_error "..."; return 3; }

    # Check tmux
    command -v tmux &>/dev/null || { log_error "..."; return 3; }

    # Load repos from config
    local repos=()
    load_all_repos repos

    # Filter to repos with uncommitted changes
    local dirty_repos=()
    for repo_spec in "${repos[@]}"; do
        local repo_path=$(repo_spec_to_path "$repo_spec")
        if [[ -d "$repo_path" ]] && has_uncommitted_changes "$repo_path"; then
            if [[ -z "$repos_filter" ]] || [[ "$repo_spec" == *"$repos_filter"* ]]; then
                dirty_repos+=("$repo_spec")
            fi
        fi
    done

    # Handle empty case
    if [[ ${#dirty_repos[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
        log_success "No repositories with uncommitted changes found."
        return 0
    fi

    # Dry run mode
    if [[ "$dry_run" == true ]]; then
        # List repos without processing
        return 0
    fi

    # Setup results tracking
    setup_agent_sweep_results

    # Handle resume/restart
    if [[ "$resume" == true ]] && load_agent_sweep_state; then
        filter_completed_repos dirty_repos
    elif [[ "$restart" == true ]]; then
        cleanup_agent_sweep_state
    fi

    # Setup trap handlers
    setup_agent_sweep_traps

    # Process (sequential or parallel)
    if [[ $parallel -gt 1 ]]; then
        run_parallel_agent_sweep dirty_repos "$parallel" "$with_release"
    else
        run_sequential_agent_sweep dirty_repos "$with_release"
    fi

    # Summary and cleanup
    print_agent_sweep_summary
    return $sweep_exit
}
```

## CLI Options to Parse
- --with-release: Enable Phase 3
- -j N, --parallel=N: Parallel workers
- --repos=PATTERN: Filter pattern
- --dry-run: Preview mode
- --resume / --restart: State recovery
- --phase{1,2,3}-timeout=N: Timeouts
- --execution-mode=MODE: plan|apply|agent
- --keep-sessions: Preserve sessions
- --keep-sessions-on-fail: Preserve failed sessions
- --attach-on-fail: Attach to first failed session for debugging
- --release-strategy=STR: Release policy
- --secret-scan=MODE: Security scanning
- --max-file-mb=N: Maximum file size limit
- --capture-lines=N: Lines to capture from pane output
- --json: JSON output mode
- --verbose / --quiet / --debug: Output control

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All CLI options parsed correctly
- [ ] Concurrent instance lock prevents multiple runs
- [ ] ntm and tmux availability checked before processing
- [ ] Repos loaded and filtered correctly
- [ ] Dry-run mode shows repos without processing
- [ ] Resume/restart state handled correctly
- [ ] Trap handlers set up for graceful shutdown
- [ ] Exit codes match ru standard (0-5)

 {"id":"bd-kips","title":"Fix installer: no releases (404) + cache bust downloads","description":"install.sh currently calls GitHub REST releases/latest which returns 404 ("Not Found") because the repo has no releases; this makes the one-liner curl|bash installer fail.

Fix:
- Detect 404/Not Found and fall back to installing ru from main (with clear warning) when no releases exist.
- Improve error messages for other API failures (rate limiting, etc.).
- Add cache-busting to download URLs (main/release asset fetches) to reduce stale caching.
- Update README/installer header examples.

Acceptance:
- One-liner install.sh works even when there are no releases.
- Curl caching issues are mitigated for downloaded artifacts.
- ShellCheck clean for changed scripts.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"GreenBeacon","created_at":"2026-01-04T21:39:01.706193748-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T21:43:37.116875117-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T21:43:37.116875117-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 047d270:
- Detect 404 response from GitHub releases/latest API
- Fall back to main branch installation with clear warnings
- Add cache-busting query params to bypass CDN/proxy caches
- Improve error messages for rate limiting (403) and other API errors
- ShellCheck clean, tested manually"}
 {"id":"bd-koxf","title":"Define review plan artifact schema and validation","description":"# Task: Define Review Plan Artifact Schema and Validation

## Purpose
Define the machine-readable JSON schema that Claude must produce for each review session. This artifact is the contract between the Plan phase and Apply phase.

## Background: Why a Structured Artifact?
- Apply phase needs to know what to do
- Can't scrape agent transcripts reliably
- Enables resume after interruption
- Provides audit trail
- Enables metrics without parsing text

## File Location
```
{worktree}/.ru/review-plan.json
```

## Schema (Version 1)

```json
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "run_id": "20250104-103000-12345",
  "repo": "owner/repo",
  "worktree_path": "/home/user/.local/state/ru/worktrees/.../owner_repo",
  
  "items": [
    {
      "type": "issue",
      "number": 42,
      "title": "Authentication fails on Windows",
      "priority": "high",
      "decision": "fix",
      "notes": "Root cause: path separator in auth.py:234",
      "risk_level": "low",
      "files_changed": ["src/auth.py"],
      "lines_changed": 5
    },
    {
      "type": "pr",
      "number": 15,
      "title": "Add Redis caching",
      "priority": "normal",
      "decision": "skip",
      "notes": "Out of scope - adds external dependency",
      "risk_level": "n/a"
    }
  ],
  
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "q1",
      "prompt": "Should I refactor all path handling or just fix this case?",
      "options": [
        {"label": "Quick fix", "description": "Fix only auth.py (5 lines)"},
        {"label": "Full refactor", "description": "Modernize all paths (45 lines)"},
        {"label": "Skip", "description": "Not a priority"}
      ],
      "recommended": "Quick fix",
      "answered": true,
      "answer": "Quick fix",
      "answered_at": "2025-01-04T10:35:00Z"
    }
  ],
  
  "git": {
    "branch": "ru/review/20250104-103000-12345/owner-repo",
    "base_ref": "main",
    "commits": [
      {
        "sha": "abc123def456",
        "subject": "Fix Windows path handling in auth.py",
        "files": ["src/auth.py"],
        "insertions": 3,
        "deletions": 2
      }
    ],
    "tests": {
      "ran": true,
      "ok": true,
      "command": "make test",
      "output_summary": "12 tests passed",
      "duration_seconds": 45
    }
  },
  
  "gh_actions": [
    {
      "op": "comment",
      "target": "issue#42",
      "body": "Fixed in commit abc123. The issue was path separators on Windows..."
    },
    {
      "op": "close",
      "target": "issue#42",
      "reason": "completed"
    },
    {
      "op": "label",
      "target": "issue#42",
      "labels": ["fixed-in-main"]
    },
    {
      "op": "comment",
      "target": "pr#15",
      "body": "Thank you for the suggestion. After review, this is out of scope..."
    }
  ],
  
  "metadata": {
    "started_at": "2025-01-04T10:30:00Z",
    "completed_at": "2025-01-04T10:45:00Z",
    "duration_seconds": 900,
    "context_usage_percent": 45,
    "model": "claude-sonnet-4",
    "driver": "local"
  }
}
```

## Field Definitions

### Top Level
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| schema_version | int | yes | Always 1 for this version |
| run_id | string | yes | Unique review run identifier |
| repo | string | yes | owner/repo format |
| worktree_path | string | yes | Absolute path to worktree |
| items | array | yes | Work items reviewed |
| questions | array | no | Questions asked/answered |
| git | object | no | Git state (may be empty if no changes) |
| gh_actions | array | no | GitHub actions to perform in Apply |
| metadata | object | yes | Session metadata |

### Item Object
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| type | "issue"\|"pr" | yes | Work item type |
| number | int | yes | Issue/PR number |
| title | string | yes | Item title |
| priority | string | yes | high/normal/low |
| decision | string | yes | fix/skip/needs-info/closed |
| notes | string | no | Explanation of decision |
| risk_level | string | no | low/medium/high/n/a |
| files_changed | array | no | Files modified for this item |
| lines_changed | int | no | Net lines changed |

### Question Object
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| id | string | yes | Unique question ID |
| prompt | string | yes | Question text |
| options | array | no | Available options |
| recommended | string | no | Agent's recommendation |
| answered | bool | yes | Whether answered |
| answer | string | no | Selected answer (if answered) |
| answered_at | string | no | ISO timestamp |

### gh_action Object
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| op | string | yes | comment/close/label/merge |
| target | string | yes | issue#N or pr#N |
| body | string | no | Comment body (for comment op) |
| reason | string | no | Close reason (for close op) |
| labels | array | no | Labels to add (for label op) |

## Validation Function
```bash
validate_review_plan() {
    local plan_file="$1"
    
    # Must exist
    [[ ! -f "$plan_file" ]] && { echo "Plan file not found"; return 1; }
    
    # Must be valid JSON
    if ! jq empty "$plan_file" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "Invalid JSON"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Required top-level fields
    if ! jq -e '.schema_version and .repo and .items' "$plan_file" >/dev/null; then
        echo "Missing required fields: schema_version, repo, or items"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Schema version check
    local version
    version=$(jq -r '.schema_version' "$plan_file")
    if [[ "$version" != "1" ]]; then
        echo "Unsupported schema version: $version"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Items must have required fields
    if ! jq -e '.items | all(.type and .number and .decision)' "$plan_file" >/dev/null; then
        echo "Items missing required fields: type, number, or decision"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Validate decision values
    local invalid_decisions
    invalid_decisions=$(jq -r '.items[] | select(.decision | IN("fix","skip","needs-info","closed") | not) | .decision' "$plan_file")
    if [[ -n "$invalid_decisions" ]]; then
        echo "Invalid decision values: $invalid_decisions"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Validate gh_actions if present
    if jq -e '.gh_actions' "$plan_file" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if ! jq -e '.gh_actions | all(.op and .target)' "$plan_file" >/dev/null; then
            echo "gh_actions missing required fields: op or target"
            return 1
        fi
    fi
    
    echo "Valid"
    return 0
}
```

## Plan Summary
```bash
summarize_review_plan() {
    local plan_file="$1"
    
    jq -r '
        "Repository: \(.repo)",
        "Items reviewed: \(.items | length)",
        "  - Fixed: \([.items[] | select(.decision == "fix")] | length)",
        "  - Skipped: \([.items[] | select(.decision == "skip")] | length)",
        "  - Needs info: \([.items[] | select(.decision == "needs-info")] | length)",
        "Commits: \(.git.commits // [] | length)",
        "Tests: \(if .git.tests.ok then "PASS" else "FAIL/NOT RUN" end)",
        "gh_actions pending: \(.gh_actions // [] | length)"
    ' "$plan_file"
}
```

## Testing
- Validate correct plan passes
- Validate missing fields rejected
- Validate invalid decisions rejected
- Parse all field types correctly
- Handle optional fields gracefully

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Schema documented with all fields
- [ ] Validation function catches all errors
- [ ] Example plans provided for testing
- [ ] Summary function works
- [ ] Optional fields handled correctly","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:37:01.268595285-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:42:10.057519193-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:42:10.057519193-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented validate_review_plan(), summarize_review_plan(), and get_review_plan_json_summary() functions in ru script. Added 14 unit tests in scripts/test_unit_review_plan.sh. All tests pass."}
 {"id":"bd-kvu5","title":"[EPIC] Error Handling & Recovery","description":"# Error Handling & Recovery

## Error Type Mapping

| Scenario | ntm Exit | ntm Error Code | ru Exit | ru Behavior |
|----------|----------|----------------|---------|-------------|
| ntm not installed | N/A | N/A | 3 | Log install command |
| tmux not installed | 2 | DEPENDENCY_MISSING | 3 | Log install advice |
| Session already exists | 1 | RESOURCE_BUSY | Skip | Kill existing, retry |
| Spawn timeout | 1 | TIMEOUT | Skip repo | Log, continue |
| Send failed | 1 | INTERNAL_ERROR | Skip repo | Log, cleanup, continue |
| Wait timeout | 1 | TIMEOUT | Skip repo | Log, cleanup, continue |
| Agent error detected | 3 | (state-based) | Skip repo | Log, cleanup, continue |
| Rate limit detected | 3 | (pattern match) | Pause | Global backoff, retry |
| Network error | 1 | (pattern match) | Skip repo | Log, continue |
| Interrupted (Ctrl+C) | N/A | N/A | 5 | Save state, cleanup |
| Preflight failed | N/A | N/A | Skip repo | Record reason, continue |
| Validation failed | N/A | N/A | Skip repo | Block commit, preserve plan |

## ntm Error Codes
| Error Code | Meaning | ru Exit Code |
|------------|---------|--------------|
| SESSION_NOT_FOUND | Session doesn't exist | 3 |
| PANE_NOT_FOUND | Pane index invalid | 3 |
| INVALID_FLAG | Bad CLI arguments | 4 |
| TIMEOUT | Wait exceeded timeout | 1 |
| INTERNAL_ERROR | Unexpected Go error | 3 |
| PERMISSION_DENIED | File/tmux permissions | 3 |
| RESOURCE_BUSY | Session locked | 1 |
| DEPENDENCY_MISSING | tmux not installed | 3 |
| NOT_IMPLEMENTED | Feature not ready | 4 |

## Rate Limit Recovery
- Detect via ntm activity (rate_limited:true) or pattern match
- Trigger global backoff (shared across all workers)
- Exponential backoff with jitter
- Max wait: 10 minutes

## Crash Recovery
- Detect via ntm wait exit code 3 or ERROR state
- Capture pane output before cleanup
- Option to preserve session for debugging

## Orphan Session Cleanup
cleanup_agent_sweep_sessions() kills all sessions matching pattern:
- ru_sweep_*_$$* or ru_sweep_*_$$_*
- Respects --keep-sessions flag

## Trap Handlers
trap 'cleanup_agent_sweep_sessions; save_agent_sweep_state "interrupted"' INT TERM

## Partial Success Handling
- Continue processing remaining repos on individual failures
- Aggregate results at end
- Exit code reflects worst case (0=all ok, 1=some failed, 2=conflicts)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:47:04.091975853-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T16:47:04.091975853-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-kvu5","depends_on_id":"bd-bx6s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:59:20.171369182-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

## Parent Epic: bd-a2wt (Testing Strategy)

## Test File
scripts/test_plan_validation.sh

## Purpose
Ensure plan validation correctly rejects invalid plans and execution works correctly for valid plans. These are critical security tests.

## Commit Plan Validation Tests

### Valid Plan Tests
- Test minimal valid commit plan structure
- Test plan with multiple commits
- Test plan with various file operations (add, modify, delete)

### Invalid Plan Tests
- Test plans with denied files (.env, id_rsa, node_modules, etc.)
- Test malformed JSON (missing fields, wrong types)
- Test shell injection attempts in message fields
- Test path traversal attempts (../ in paths)

## Commit Plan Execution Tests
- Test successful commit creation
- Test multi-commit execution in order
- Test new file addition
- Test file modification
- Test handling of execution failures

## Release Plan Validation Tests
- Test valid release plan acceptance
- Test duplicate tag rejection
- Test invalid version format rejection (non-semver)
- Test title/body length limits
- Test missing changelog rejection

## Logging Requirements
Every test MUST:
1. Call log_test_start() with test name
2. Use log_verbose() for each check
3. Use log_success() or log_error() for results
4. Report assertion counts

## Related Beads
- Tests: bd-8bxp (validate_commit_plan)
- Tests: bd-prgk (execute_commit_plan)
- Tests: bd-u2t8 (validate/execute release plan)
- Parent epic: bd-a2wt (Testing Strategy)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Valid commit plans pass validation
- [ ] Denied files in plans are rejected
- [ ] Malformed JSON is rejected
- [ ] Shell injection attempts are blocked
- [ ] Path traversal attempts are blocked
- [ ] Commit execution creates correct commits
- [ ] Valid release plans pass validation
- [ ] Duplicate tags are rejected
- [ ] Invalid version formats are rejected

 {"id":"bd-s3iy","title":"Implement non-interactive mode for CI/automation","description":"# Task: Implement Non-Interactive Mode

## Purpose
Add --non-interactive flag to `ru review` for CI/automation environments where no human is available to answer questions.

## Background
From AGENTS.md:
- Non-interactive mode (--non-interactive) suppresses prompts for CI/automation
- Must work in headless environments (no TTY)

## Non-Interactive Behavior

### Question Handling
When a question arises in non-interactive mode:

1. **AskUserQuestion with recommended option**: Auto-select recommended
2. **AskUserQuestion without recommended**: Log warning, skip item
3. **Agent text question**: Log warning, skip item
4. **External prompt (password, conflict)**: Fail with exit code 3

### Implementation

```bash
handle_question_non_interactive() {
    local question_info="$1"
    local session_id="$2"
    
    local reason recommended
    reason=$(echo "$question_info" | jq -r .reason)
    recommended=$(echo "$question_info" | jq -r .recommended // "")
    
    case "$reason" in
        ask_user_question)
            if [[ -n "$recommended" ]]; then
                log_info "[non-interactive] Auto-selecting: $recommended"
                driver_send_to_session "$session_id" "$recommended"
                return 0
            else
                log_warn "[non-interactive] No recommended option, skipping item"
                record_item_outcome "$repo_id" "issue" "$number" "skipped" \
                    "Skipped in non-interactive mode (no recommended option)"
                driver_send_to_session "$session_id" "skip"
                return 0
            fi
            ;;
        agent_question_text)
            log_warn "[non-interactive] Agent text question, skipping"
            driver_send_to_session "$session_id" "skip"
            return 0
            ;;
        external_prompt)
            log_error "[non-interactive] External prompt requires human - failing"
            return 3
            ;;
    esac
}
```

### Auto-Answer Policy

Configurable via:
```bash
REVIEW_NON_INTERACTIVE_POLICY="auto"  # auto|skip|fail
```

- **auto**: Use recommended if available, skip otherwise
- **skip**: Always skip questions (log them for later)
- **fail**: Fail immediately on any question (strictest)

### Question Logging

In non-interactive mode, log all questions for later review:
```bash
log_skipped_question() {
    local question_info="$1"
    local log_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/skipped-questions-$REVIEW_RUN_ID.json"
    
    echo "$question_info" >> "$log_file"
}
```

### TTY Detection

```bash
check_interactive_capability() {
    # Check if stdin is a terminal
    if [[ \! -t 0 ]]; then
        if [[ "$REVIEW_NON_INTERACTIVE" \!= "true" ]]; then
            log_warn "No TTY detected, enabling --non-interactive mode"
            REVIEW_NON_INTERACTIVE=true
        fi
    fi
}
```

### Summary Output

In non-interactive mode, output a summary of skipped questions:
```
Review completed with 3 skipped questions.
Questions logged to: ~/.local/state/ru/skipped-questions-20250104-103000.json

Use "ru review --resume --interactive" to answer remaining questions.
```

## Flags

```bash
--non-interactive     # Enable non-interactive mode
--auto-answer=POLICY  # Policy: auto (default), skip, fail
```

## Integration Points
- Detect non-interactive at startup
- Handle questions differently based on mode
- Log skipped questions for later
- Provide resume path for answering questions later

## Testing
- Verify TTY detection works
- Verify auto-answer selects recommended
- Verify skip logs questions
- Verify fail mode exits on question
- Verify resume path works

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] --non-interactive flag works
- [ ] TTY auto-detection works
- [ ] Auto-answer uses recommended options
- [ ] Skipped questions logged
- [ ] Clear summary output
- [ ] --resume allows answering skipped questions later

 {"id":"bd-taoo","title":"Write state management and resume tests","description":"Implements detailed tests for state management, resume, and artifact capture.

## Parent Epic: bd-a2wt (Testing Strategy)

## Test File
scripts/test_state_management.sh

## Purpose
Verify state persistence works correctly for resume/restart functionality. Critical for handling interruptions gracefully.

## State File Tests

### State File Creation
- Test state file created at correct location
- Test state file contains all required fields
- Test state file is valid JSON

### State File Updates
- Test state updates as repos complete
- Test failed repos recorded with error reason
- Test timing information captured

### State File Schema
Required fields:
- started_at: ISO timestamp
- repos: array of repo states
- completed_count: number
- failed_count: number
- current_phase: string
- worker_pids: array (parallel mode)

## Resume Tests

### Basic Resume
- Test --resume detects existing state
- Test completed repos are skipped
- Test failed repos are retried
- Test progress continues from last position

### Resume After Interruption
- Simulate Ctrl+C during processing
- Verify state file is complete
- Test resume picks up correctly

### Resume Validation
- Test resume rejects corrupted state
- Test resume warns on stale state (> 24h)
- Test --restart clears state and starts fresh

## Artifact Capture Tests

### Git State Capture
- Test git_status_before captured
- Test git_status_after captured
- Test diff between states computed

### Agent Output Capture
- Test pane_tail captured
- Test commit_plan JSON saved
- Test release_plan JSON saved (if present)

### Artifact Storage
- Test artifacts stored per-repo
- Test artifact directory structure correct
- Test large outputs truncated appropriately

## Logging Requirements
- State file writes logged
- Resume decisions logged
- Artifact capture operations logged
- Any skipped repos explained

- Tests: bd-7x6h (artifact capture)
- Parent epic: bd-a2wt (Testing Strategy)

- [ ] State updates atomically (no partial writes)
- [ ] Resume skips completed repos
- [ ] Resume retries failed repos
- [ ] Interrupted runs leave valid state
- [ ] Artifacts captured for each processed repo
- [ ] Large artifacts handled gracefully

 {"id":"bd-tcns","title":"Design agent prompts for review workflow","description":"Task: Design Agent Prompts for Review Workflow

Purpose
-------
Craft the prompts that instruct Claude on how to review issues/PRs, what
constraints to follow (no gh mutations), and what artifacts to produce.

Background: Prompt Engineering Matters
--------------------------------------
The quality of automated reviews depends heavily on prompt design:
- Clear policy communication (no PRs accepted)
- Explicit constraints (Plan mode = no mutations)
- Required output format (review-plan.json)
- Independent verification emphasis

Prompt Components
-----------------

1. System Context (AGENTS.md Reading)
   First read ALL of AGENTS.md and README.md carefully.
   Use your code investigation agent mode to fully understand the code,
   technical architecture, and purpose of the project. Use ultrathink.

2. Digest Handling
   If prior digest exists: read it, update based on delta commits
   If no digest: create comprehensive fresh digest
   Always write updated digest to .ru/repo-digest.md

3. Policy Communication
   POLICY: We do not accept PRs or outside contributions. The maintainer
   policy is disclosed to users. Claude reviews and independently decides
   whether and how to address submissions. Bug reports are welcome.

4. Review Instructions
   For each work item:
   - Read via gh issue view or gh pr view
   - Verify claims independently - do not trust user reports blindly
   - Check dates against recent commits (many issues may be stale)
   - If actionable: create local commits with fixes/features
   - If unclear: prepare question for maintainer

5. Plan Mode Constraints (CRITICAL)
   - DO NOT run gh issue comment, gh issue close, gh pr comment, etc.
   - DO NOT push any changes
   - Only use gh for READ operations (view, list)
   - All mutations will be applied by ru in a separate phase

6. Required Output
   Must produce .ru/review-plan.json with schema:
   {
     "schema_version": 1,
     "run_id": "...",
     "repo": "owner/repo",
     "items": [...],
     "questions": [...],
     "git": {...},
     "gh_actions": [...]
   }

Prompt Template Function
------------------------
generate_review_prompt() takes:
- wt_path: worktree path
- repo_name: owner/repo format
- work_items: JSON array of items to review
- run_id: unique review run ID

Checks for existing digest and adjusts instructions accordingly.

Prompt Variations
-----------------
- Quick Review: Focus on high-priority items, target 10 minutes
- Deep Review: Comprehensive analysis, architectural implications
- Security-Focused: Security items first, verify all claims

Testing
-------
- Verify Claude produces valid review-plan.json
- Verify gh mutations are NOT attempted
- Verify digest is created/updated
- Verify questions use AskUserQuestion tool

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Prompt template produces working reviews
- [ ] Plan mode constraints enforced
- [ ] Review plan artifact created correctly
- [ ] Policy communicated clearly
- [ ] Digest handling works both cases
- [ ] Questions formatted for aggregation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:37:17.313860817-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:44:47.540328214-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:44:47.540328214-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-tcns","depends_on_id":"bd-koxf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:44:54.883229859-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-u2t8","title":"Implement release plan validation and execution","description":"Implements validate_release_plan() and execute_release_plan() functions per the NTM integration plan.

## Background

After Phase 3, the agent produces a release plan JSON. Before any release operations, ru MUST validate this plan against security rules. This mirrors the commit plan validation but handles release-specific concerns like version formats and changelog entries.

## Functions to Implement

### validate_release_plan()

Purpose: Validate the release plan JSON against safety rules before execution.

Inputs:
- plan_json: The raw release plan from the agent
- repo_path: Path to the repository

Validation Rules:
1. version must match semver format (vX.Y.Z or X.Y.Z)
2. changelog must exist and contain expected version header
3. tag_name must not already exist
4. title must be < 200 chars
5. body must be < 10000 chars
6. files array must pass file denylist check
7. No shell metacharacters in any string field

Returns: 0 if valid, non-zero with error messages on stderr if invalid.

### execute_release_plan()

Purpose: Execute a validated release plan using gh CLI and git commands.

Steps:
1. Verify plan was validated (guard against direct calls)
2. Create and push the git tag to origin (two-step: create locally, then transmit to remote)
3. Create GitHub release with gh release create
4. Upload any specified release assets
5. Capture release URL for reporting

Error Handling:
- If tag creation fails, abort and report
- If release creation fails, attempt cleanup of tag
- Log all operations for audit trail

## Implementation Notes

- Uses gh release create for GitHub release creation
- Tag transmission to remote uses standard git remote operations
- All string fields must be shell-escaped before use
- Follows the Planner->Validator->Executor pattern

## Related Beads

- Depends on: bd-8bxp (validate_commit_plan - similar pattern)
- Depends on: bd-y3vd (has_release_workflow detection)
- Parent epic: bd-jk4n (Security Guardrails)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] validate_release_plan() rejects invalid version formats
- [ ] validate_release_plan() rejects existing tags
- [ ] validate_release_plan() enforces string length limits
- [ ] execute_release_plan() creates tag and release atomically
- [ ] execute_release_plan() handles partial failures gracefully
- [ ] All operations logged for audit trail","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:21:36.975238928-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T23:18:46.33407341-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-u2t8","depends_on_id":"bd-8bxp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:27:19.309161247-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-u2t8","depends_on_id":"bd-y3vd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:27:19.338995792-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

# Parallel processing

# Dry run

|------|---------|
| 0 | Success |
| 1 | Partial failure |
| 2 | Conflicts |
| 3 | Dependency error |
| 4 | Invalid arguments |
| 5 | Interrupted |

### Security Section

- File denylist enforcement
- Size limits

### Troubleshooting Section
Common issues and solutions.

- Environment variables

 {"id":"bd-vc7e","title":"Fix installer self-refresh when piped (stdin BASH_SOURCE empty)","description":"Problem: when install.sh is executed from stdin (curl | bash), BASH_SOURCE[0] can be empty, so maybe_self_refresh_installer() fails to detect stdin and never performs its cache-busting self-refresh. This can leave users running a stale cached installer (including older logic that queries GitHub API for latest version).

Fix:
- Treat empty BASH_SOURCE[0] + non-tty stdin as stdin execution and proceed with self-refresh.
- Keep existing opt-out via RU_INSTALLER_NO_SELF_REFRESH and recursion guard RU_INSTALLER_REFRESHED.

Acceptance:
- curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/.../install.sh | bash triggers installer self-refresh and uses cache-busting URL.
- ShellCheck warning+ passes for install.sh.

 {"id":"bd-vcr9","title":"Implement gh_actions execution from review plan","description":"Task: Implement gh_actions Execution

Purpose
-------
Execute GitHub mutations (comments, closes, labels) from the gh_actions
array in review-plan.json during the Apply phase.

gh_actions Schema
-----------------
{
  "gh_actions": [
    {"op": "comment", "target": "issue#42", "body": "Fixed in..."},
    {"op": "close", "target": "issue#42", "reason": "completed"},
    {"op": "label", "target": "issue#42", "labels": ["fixed-in-main"]},
    {"op": "comment", "target": "pr#15", "body": "Thank you..."}
  ]
}

Supported Operations
--------------------

1. comment
   Add comment to issue or PR
   gh issue comment N --body "..."
   gh pr comment N --body "..."

2. close
   Close issue or PR
   gh issue close N --reason "..."
   gh pr close N --comment "..."

3. label
   Add labels to issue
   gh issue edit N --add-label "label1,label2"

4. (merge - NOT SUPPORTED per policy)

Implementation
--------------

execute_gh_actions()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local plan_file="$2"
  
  local actions
  actions=$(jq -c .gh_actions[] "$plan_file" 2>/dev/null) || return 0
  
  while IFS= read -r action; do
    local op target
    op=$(echo "$action" | jq -r .op)
    target=$(echo "$action" | jq -r .target)
    
    case "$op" in
      comment) execute_comment "$repo_id" "$action" ;;
      close)   execute_close "$repo_id" "$action" ;;
      label)   execute_label "$repo_id" "$action" ;;
      *)
        log_warn "Unknown gh_action op: $op"
        ;;
    esac
  done <<< "$actions"

execute_comment()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local action="$2"
  
  local target body type number
  target=$(echo "$action" | jq -r .target)
  body=$(echo "$action" | jq -r .body)
  
  # Parse target: "issue#42" or "pr#15"
  type="${target%%#*}"
  number="${target##*#}"
  
  log_step "Commenting on $target"
  
  case "$type" in
    issue)
      gh issue comment "$number" -R "$repo_id" --body "$body"
      ;;
    pr)
      gh pr comment "$number" -R "$repo_id" --body "$body"
      ;;
  esac
  
  record_action_executed "$repo_id" "$action"

execute_close()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local action="$2"
  
  local target reason type number
  target=$(echo "$action" | jq -r .target)
  reason=$(echo "$action" | jq -r ".reason // \"completed\"")
  type="${target%%#*}"
  number="${target##*#}"
  
  log_step "Closing $target"
  
  case "$type" in
    issue)
      gh issue close "$number" -R "$repo_id" --reason "$reason"
      ;;
    pr)
      gh pr close "$number" -R "$repo_id" --comment "Closing: $reason"
      ;;
  esac
  
  record_action_executed "$repo_id" "$action"

execute_label()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local action="$2"
  
  local target labels_json type number
  target=$(echo "$action" | jq -r .target)
  labels_json=$(echo "$action" | jq -r ".labels | join(\",\")")
  type="${target%%#*}"
  number="${target##*#}"
  
  log_step "Adding labels to $target: $labels_json"
  
  gh issue edit "$number" -R "$repo_id" --add-label "$labels_json"
  
  record_action_executed "$repo_id" "$action"

Action Recording
----------------
Track what was executed for audit:

record_action_executed()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local action="$2"
  
  local log_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/gh_actions.log"
  local ts=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
  
  echo "${ts}|${repo_id}|${action}" >> "$log_file"

Error Handling
--------------
- Log errors but continue with other actions
- Record failed actions in state
- Retry logic for transient failures
- Skip already-executed actions on resume

Testing
-------
- Mock gh commands for unit tests
- Verify comment body escaping
- Verify close reasons work
- Verify labels applied
- Test error handling

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Comments posted correctly
- [ ] Issues/PRs closed with reason
- [ ] Labels applied
- [ ] Actions logged for audit
- [ ] Errors handled gracefully
- [ ] Idempotent on retry","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:42:09.158053493-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T18:32:04.738399665-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T18:32:04.738399665-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented gh_actions execution with 9 functions: execute_gh_actions, execute_gh_action_{comment,close,label}, gh_action_already_executed, record_gh_action_log, parse_gh_action_target, canonicalize_gh_action, get_gh_actions_log_file. Features idempotence, audit logging, policy enforcement.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vcr9","depends_on_id":"bd-ldyv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:14.518493177-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
